title: Blue Cheese Turnovers
summary: We've made these rich and tangy blue cheese turnovers using just six ingredients. A great one for snacking on during the weekend.

method.description: Heat the oven to 180°C. Tip the walnuts onto a tray and roast for 10 mins, then remove from the oven and drizzle with half of the honey. Add a pinch of salt and set aside to cool.
Cut the pears in half, then use a sharp knife to cut away the core. Slice half the pears. Finely chop the rest of the pears and set aside in a bowl.

Roll out the puff pastry sheet and cut into 6 squares. Take each square and lie a slice of prosciutto from one of the corners to the opposite corner. Top with a few slices of the pear, followed by the blue cheese. Fold the other two corners of the square over the filling to form a turnover shape. Place on a baking sheet and repeat with the rest of the puff squares. Drizzle with a little honey and bake for 15-20 mins, until golden.
Meanwhile, make the pear salsa. Add olive oil to the finely chopped pears, a drizzle of honey and a big pinch of salt and pepper.
Serve the turnovers, topped with the pear salsa and chopped walnuts.

chefs.name: Elena Silcock
chefs.summary: Elena Silcock is the Managing food editor at Mob. She's also a food writer, chef and food stylist who has worked for various brands including BBC Good Food and Olive Magazine as well as in the kitchens of restaurants in both London and Copenhagen.
